title.rendered: Unveiled: New Super-Secure and Faster Currency Payment Option

content.rendered:
TravelFX is taking its role in providing your holiday money to the next level with the launch of a brand new, quick and safe payment method.

From today, you can use the FREE ‘Pay with Bank’ system on the TravelFX website – a payment option you may have seen when shopping with some of your favourite online retailers.

How does it work?

It is now even simpler to pay than ever before. When you are making your travel money order, select the ‘Pay with Bank’ option. At the payment stage, you will be able to connect directly with your online banking account and all the order details will be populated for you. All you have to do is authorise the payment.

If you are ordering through your computer or laptop but would prefer to pay via your mobile banking app, don’t worry, there’s a QR code that you can scan with your phone that will arrange the payment through your mobile phone, just the same as on your computer. All you need to do is authorise the transaction. It is so quick, simple and safe.

There will be no more taking time to input all of your credit card details or having to manually transfer from your bank account to ours. This revolutionary way to pay will save you time, guarantee a safe transaction and you will have your currency delivered in our usual timely manner.

Travel FX’s Graham Tennant said: “Some of the biggest names in online retail have started using this system for its shoppers, so it made sense for us to offer ‘Pay by Bank’ so you can enjoy a quicker and safer experience using our online store.

This new payment option will transform the way you buy your foreign currency. And, because you don’t have to input credit card details or account details for bank transfers, this is one of the safest and most secure ways of paying for your holiday money.

“Of course, you can still choose to use the traditional way of paying for currency if you prefer. Everyone will still get the trusted and reliable service they are used to from TravelFX. Customers can still select the day they want their money to be delivered, and it will arrive with them securely as a Royal Mail Recorded Delivery on the day they specify,” he added.

Simple and Secure Payments

Pay by Bank powered by Vyne, which is authorised and regulated by the FCA (Financial Conduct Authority) as an Authorised Payment Institute, uses your bank’s own technology to carry out the transaction. At no time is your personal data shared with Vyne and the great advantage of this form of payment is not having to input card and bank account details, so you are less likely to fall victim to fraud.

What does the Money Saving Expert Say?

The Money Saving Expert has reviewed this form of payment and says: “Technically, banks share your information securely via a technology called application programming interfaces (APIs). APIs simply allow two providers to ‘talk’ to each other and pass the information you’ve given permission to share.”

The article added: “As long as they’re authorised, providers will only be able to access data needed for the service you’ve signed up to.”

This latest advance in our business is part of our continued commitment to you to provide five-star service and the very best rates for travel money online.

itemListElement.name: Unveiled: New Super-Secure and Faster Currency Payment Option

@graph.name: Travel FX Blog
@graph.description: Travel money news and tips from Travel FX

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
